lede: A look at April 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Guy Verhofstadt and Thiago Alcântara, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Borussia Dortmund
events.desc: The tour bus of the German football team Borussia Dortmund is attacked with roadside bombs in Dortmund, Germany. Three bombs exploded as the bus ferried the team to the Westfalenstadion for the first leg of their quarter-final against Monaco.

events.title: 2011 Minsk Metro bombing
events.desc: An explosion in the Minsk Metro, Belarus, kills 15 people and injures 204 others.

events.title: 2002 Venezuelan coup attempt
events.desc: Over two hundred thousand people march in Caracas towards the presidential palace to demand the resignation of President Hugo Chávez. Nineteen protesters are killed.

events.title: Rudi Dutschke
events.desc: A failed assassination attempt on Rudi Dutschke, leader of the German student movement, leaves Dutschke suffering from brain damage.

events.title: Pope John XXIII
events.desc: Pope John XXIII issues Pacem in terris, the first encyclical addressed to all Christians instead of only Catholics, and which described the conditions for world peace in human terms.

events.title: SMS Blücher
events.desc: SMS Blücher, the last armored cruiser to be built by the Imperial German Navy, is launched.

events.title: Treaty of Fontainebleau (1814)
events.desc: The Treaty of Fontainebleau ends the War of the Sixth Coalition against Napoleon Bonaparte, and forces him to abdicate unconditionally for the first time.

events.title: Peace of Utrecht
events.desc: France and Great Britain sign the Treaty of Utrecht, bringing an end to the War of the Spanish Succession (Queen Anne's War). Britain accepts Philip V as King of Spain, while Philip renounces any claim to the French throne.

events.title: William III of England
events.desc: William III and Mary II are crowned as joint sovereigns of Great Britain on the same day that the Scottish Parliament concurs with the English decision of 12 February.

births.name: Guy Verhofstadt
births.line: Belgian politician, 47th Prime Minister of Belgium

births.name: Anton LaVey
births.line: American occultist, founded the Church of Satan

deaths.text: Jonathan Winters, American comedian, actor and screenwriter
Kurt Vonnegut, American novelist, short story writer, and playwright
Anawrahta, king of Burma and founder of the Pagan Empire
Emperor Yang of Sui, Chinese emperor of the Sui Dynasty

observances.text: Christian feast day: Antipas of Pergamum (Greek Orthodox Church)
